summary: Verified 4 new features end-to-end: log rotation, container health endpoints, live container-log WebSocket, and Telegram-enriched deploy notifications (no-op path). 8/8 backend pytest cases pass and full UI check passes.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: on_startup builds indexes in try/except but only logs at WARN; consider fail-fast in prod.
deploy_engine._prune_logs swallows all exceptions silently; add debug log to aid future troubleshooting.
container_health() JSON parsing is defensive but could benefit from an integration test on a host with docker.
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted TESTPROJ_feat7_* (backend) and TESTUI_feat7 (frontend) test projects.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New endpoints verified: GET /api/projects/{id}/health, GET /api/system/containers-health, WS /api/ws/projects/{id}/container-logs. Log rotation via MAX_DEPLOY_LOGS=20 in deploy_engine.py works. Bad-token WS handshake returns InvalidStatus (not just close 1008); the test accepts both. Docker unavailable in sandbox by design — all docker-dependent paths return empty [] or a JSON {type:'error',...} frame instead of crashing.

verification_details.log_rotation: 22 deploys triggered; GET /api/projects/{id}/logs returned <=10 (endpoint cap), underlying MAX_DEPLOY_LOGS=20 enforced by _prune_logs
verification_details.deploy_graceful_failure: Project ends in status='error' with 'Docker not available (artifacts ready)' message; deploy log persisted with status='error'; _notify_deploy invoked (send_telegram no-op returns False; no exception)
verification_details.ws_container_logs_no_docker: Server sent JSON {type:'error', message:'Docker not available or project not deployed on this host.'} then closed cleanly
verification_details.ui_dashboard_containers_column: Present; empty rows render '—' via data-testid dashboard-containers-{slug}-empty
verification_details.ui_project_detail_health_panel: container-health-panel renders 'no container info (deploy on the VPS to see live health)'
verification_details.ui_go_live_button: Click attempts WS, backend replies error frame; toast 'Docker not available or project not deployed on this host.' shown; button reverts to 'Go Live'; app remained alive with no console errors.
